articles.headline: India Unveils Ambitious Roadmap for Green Hydrogen Adoption, Targets Energy Security
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) released a detailed roadmap outlining strategies for scaling up green hydrogen production and consumption across various sectors.
• The plan emphasizes incentives for domestic manufacturing of electrolysers, development of green hydrogen hubs, and R&D for cost reduction.
• Focus areas include steel, refineries, fertilizer, and mobility sectors for initial adoption, aiming to reduce dependence on fossil fuels.
• The roadmap also highlights India's potential to become a global hub for green hydrogen export, leveraging its renewable energy resources.
• Public-private partnerships and international collaborations are key components for achieving the mission's ambitious targets.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM) launched in 2023.
Target sectors: steel, refineries, fertilizer, mobility.
Key component: domestic manufacturing of electrolysers.
MNRE is the nodal ministry for NGHM.
Goal is to reduce fossil fuel imports and achieve net-zero targets.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Evaluate the role of government policies and private investment in fostering a robust green hydrogen ecosystem.
Examine the potential economic benefits, including job creation and export opportunities, from the NGHM.
Critically assess the technological hurdles and infrastructure requirements for widespread green hydrogen implementation.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Transparency in Electoral Funding, Reiterates Disclosure Mandate
articles.explainer:
• In a landmark ruling, the Supreme Court of India reinforced the necessity of transparency in political funding by upholding the disclosure requirements related to electoral bonds.
• The judgment emphasized that the right to information of the voter is paramount, ensuring that citizens are aware of the sources of political party funding.
• The ruling addressed petitions seeking clarity and enforcement of previous directives regarding the complete disclosure of electoral bond data.
• It directed the Election Commission of India (ECI) to ensure the timely publication of all donor and recipient details, without any obfuscation.
• This decision is expected to strengthen democratic processes and accountability in political financing.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
prelimsPoints: Electoral Bonds Scheme was introduced in 2017.
Supreme Court previously struck down anonymity in electoral bonds in February 2024.
Right to Information is a fundamental right under Article 19(1)(a).
Election Commission of India (ECI) is responsible for regulating elections.
ADR is a key petitioner in electoral funding cases.
mainsPoints: Analyze the impact of the Supreme Court's verdict on transparency and accountability in India's electoral system.
Discuss the arguments for and against the anonymity of electoral bonds in the context of political funding and donor privacy.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in strengthening democratic institutions and ensuring fair elections in India.
Examine the challenges in implementing complete transparency in political funding and suggest further reforms.
Compare India's electoral finance regulations with global best practices and identify areas for improvement.
articles.imageDescription: Image of the Supreme Court of India building.
articles.trivia: The first general elections in independent India were held from 1951 to 1952, marking the largest democratic exercise at the time.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Global Headwinds, Focuses on Inflation Targeting
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, deciding to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at X%.
• The decision was influenced by persistent global economic uncertainties, geopolitical tensions, and the need to anchor domestic inflation expectations.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth, maintaining an 'accommodative stance with withdrawal of accommodation'.
• RBI Governor highlighted the resilience of the Indian economy but cautioned about the potential impact of crude oil prices and supply chain disruptions.
• Future policy actions will remain data-dependent, with a close watch on inflation trajectory and growth momentum.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
articles.source: Reserve Bank of India / Business Standard
prelimsPoints: Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of 6 members (3 RBI, 3 GoI).
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Inflation target set by the government in consultation with RBI is 4% (+/- 2%).
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
'Withdrawal of accommodation' stance indicates focus on reducing liquidity.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its implications for the Indian economy.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with the objective of supporting economic growth.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in addressing supply-side inflation and global economic shocks.
Examine the transmission mechanism of monetary policy in India and the role of commercial banks.
Critically assess the 'accommodative stance with withdrawal of accommodation' and its future trajectory.
articles.imageDescription: Facade of the Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai.
articles.trivia: The RBI was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, initially headquartered in Kolkata.

articles.headline: India-EU Summit Strengthens Strategic Partnership with Focus on Digital and Green Transition
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-European Union (EU) Strategic Partnership Dialogue concluded with both sides reaffirming their commitment to a robust and comprehensive partnership.
• Key outcomes included enhanced cooperation on digital technologies, including AI, cybersecurity, and data governance, alongside joint efforts on green energy transition.
• Discussions also covered trade and investment facilitation, supply chain resilience, and collaboration on critical raw materials.
• Leaders emphasized the importance of a free, open, and rules-based Indo-Pacific, with India and the EU playing crucial roles.
• Agreements were signed to boost research and innovation, particularly in areas like sustainable urban development and clean energy technologies.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
articles.source: Ministry of External Affairs / The Times of India
prelimsPoints: India and EU established a Strategic Partnership in 2004.
EU is India's third-largest trading partner.
Both sides are negotiating a Free Trade Agreement (FTA).
Key areas of cooperation: trade, climate, digital, security.
EU's Global Gateway strategy aims to counter China's BRI.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of the India-EU partnership in the current geopolitical landscape.
Discuss the potential for cooperation between India and the EU in driving the global digital and green transitions.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in accelerating the India-EU Free Trade Agreement negotiations.
Examine how India and the EU can collaborate to promote a stable and prosperous Indo-Pacific region.
Assess the role of multilateralism and rules-based order in the India-EU joint vision.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union displayed together during a summit.
articles.trivia: The European Union is a unique economic and political union of 27 member states, covering much of the continent.

articles.headline: Veteran Labor Activist Baba Adhav Passes Away at 95, Leaves Legacy of Social Justice
articles.explainer:
• Noted social and labor activist Baba Adhav passed away at the age of 95, leaving behind a profound legacy of advocating for the rights of unorganized workers and the marginalized.
• A staunch proponent of social justice, Adhav dedicated his life to organizing and empowering rickshaw pullers, headload workers, and other informal sector laborers in Maharashtra.
• He was a prominent figure in various social movements, including the anti-liquor movement and campaigns against exploitation of the poor.
• Adhav was also a prolific author, using his writings to articulate the struggles and demands of the working class and promote secular values.
• His demise marks the end of an era for grassroots activism and a significant loss for the social justice movement in India.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Effects of globalization on Indian society.
Social empowerment, communalism, regionalism & secularism.
prelimsPoints: Baba Adhav was known for his work with unorganized laborers.
He primarily worked in Maharashtra.
His activism covered labor rights, anti-liquor movements, and social justice.
He was also an author.
His work is relevant to the study of social movements in post-independence India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the contributions of Baba Adhav to the labor movement and social justice in India.
Analyze the challenges faced by unorganized sector workers in India and the role of activists like Adhav in addressing them.
Evaluate the effectiveness of grassroots social movements in bringing about policy changes and social empowerment.
Examine the enduring relevance of secular values and social equity in contemporary Indian society, as championed by Adhav.
Critically assess the impact of eminent personalities on shaping public discourse and government policies related to marginalized sections.
articles.imageDescription: Black and white portrait of Baba Adhav.
articles.trivia: The first organized labor strike in India occurred in 1877 at the Empress Mills, Nagpur, over wage issues.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite for Climate Monitoring
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched a new generation Earth Observation Satellite (EOS) from Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota.
• The satellite, named EOS-XX, is equipped with advanced remote sensing instruments designed to monitor climate change indicators, agricultural health, and disaster preparedness.
• This mission is crucial for enhancing India's capabilities in environmental monitoring, resource management, and providing critical data for meteorological forecasts.
• The satellite will provide high-resolution imagery and data, aiding in understanding land-use patterns, forest cover changes, and water resource management.
• The launch further solidifies India's position in global space technology and its commitment to utilizing space for societal benefits.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: ISRO is India's national space agency.
Earth Observation Satellites (EOS) are used for monitoring Earth's surface.
Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC) is India's primary spaceport.
Remote sensing involves collecting information about an object or area without being in direct contact with it.
PSLV and GSLV are ISRO's operational launch vehicles.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites for India's climate resilience and resource management.
Analyze how ISRO's advancements in space technology contribute to India's developmental goals and global standing.
Evaluate the role of remote sensing data in disaster prediction, early warning systems, and post-disaster assessment.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India in developing and deploying advanced space-based applications.
Critically assess the importance of indigenous space capabilities for national security and economic growth.
articles.imageDescription: Rocket launching from a launchpad with smoke trails, against a clear sky.
articles.trivia: India's first satellite, Aryabhata, was launched on April 19, 1975, by the Soviet Union.

articles.headline: New Conservation Plan Rolled Out for Critically Endangered Species in Western Ghats
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) launched a comprehensive conservation plan targeting critically endangered species in the Western Ghats.
• The initiative focuses on habitat restoration, anti-poaching measures, community participation, and scientific research for species like the Malabar Civet and Lion-tailed Macaque.
• The plan integrates traditional ecological knowledge with modern conservation techniques, involving local communities as key stakeholders.
• Funding will be allocated for establishing new wildlife corridors, reducing human-wildlife conflict, and strengthening protected area management.
• This move underscores India's commitment to biodiversity preservation in one of the world's top biodiversity hotspots.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change / Down To Earth
prelimsPoints: Western Ghats is one of 8 global biodiversity hotspots.
Malabar Civet and Lion-tailed Macaque are endemic to Western Ghats.
IUCN Red List classifies species based on extinction risk.
Wildlife Protection Act, 1972 is the primary law for wildlife conservation in India.
Community participation is crucial for successful conservation efforts.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats and the threats it faces from anthropogenic activities.
Analyze the key features of the new conservation plan and its potential effectiveness in protecting endangered species.
Evaluate the role of community participation and traditional knowledge in biodiversity conservation efforts.
Examine the challenges in implementing conservation strategies in diverse and densely populated regions like the Western Ghats.
Suggest additional measures to strengthen biodiversity protection and mitigate human-wildlife conflict in India.
articles.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with dense forests and a waterfall.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats is older than the Himalayas, and its forests influence the Indian monsoon weather patterns.

articles.headline: Indigenous Scorpene-Class Submarine 'Vagir' Inducted into Indian Navy, Boosting Maritime Security
articles.explainer:
• The fifth Kalvari-class (Scorpene-class) submarine, INS Vagir, was formally commissioned into the Indian Navy, significantly enhancing India's underwater warfare capabilities.
• Built under Project 75 by Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Limited (MDL) in collaboration with Naval Group of France, Vagir demonstrates India's growing self-reliance in defense manufacturing.
• The submarine is designed for anti-surface warfare, anti-submarine warfare, intelligence gathering, mine laying, and area surveillance.
• Its induction is part of a broader strategy to modernize the Indian Navy and strengthen its presence in the Indian Ocean Region.
• This development underscores the 'Make in India' initiative in defense and India's commitment to becoming a net security provider in the region.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: INS Vagir is a Scorpene-class submarine.
It is built under Project 75.
MDL is the primary builder in India.
Naval Group of France is the collaborator.
Scorpene submarines are conventional diesel-electric attack submarines.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous submarine construction for India's maritime security and strategic autonomy.
Analyze the role of the Indian Navy in safeguarding India's economic interests and maintaining stability in the Indian Ocean Region.
Evaluate the progress and challenges of the 'Make in India' initiative in the defense sector, using Project 75 as an example.
Examine the geopolitical implications of strengthening India's underwater warfare capabilities for regional power dynamics.
Suggest measures to further accelerate defense indigenization and reduce reliance on foreign military imports.
articles.imageDescription: INS Vagir submarine sailing on the sea surface with naval personnel on board.
articles.trivia: The first Indian Navy submarine, INS Kalvari (F-class), was commissioned on December 8, 1967, from the Soviet Union.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Focus on Skill Development and Vocational Training for Youth
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a progress report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, highlighting significant strides in integrating skill development.
• The report emphasized the expansion of vocational education from school to higher education levels, with a focus on practical, industry-relevant skills.
• New guidelines were issued for collaboration between educational institutions and industries to provide apprenticeships and on-the-job training opportunities.
• The policy aims to bridge the skill gap in the workforce, enhance employability, and foster an entrepreneurial mindset among the youth.
• Efforts are underway to align curriculum with National Skills Qualification Framework (NSQF) and introduce credit-based vocational courses.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
articles.source: Ministry of Education / Press Information Bureau (PIB)
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
One of its key pillars is vocational education integration.
NSQF organizes qualifications by a series of levels of knowledge, skills, and aptitude.
The policy aims for a multidisciplinary and holistic education system.
GER in higher education is a key target metric for NEP.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of NEP 2020 in addressing India's skill gap and enhancing youth employability.
Discuss the challenges in integrating vocational education effectively into the mainstream curriculum from school to higher education.
Evaluate the role of industry-academia collaboration in making skill development programs more relevant and outcome-oriented.
Examine how NEP 2020 can foster an entrepreneurial ecosystem and contribute to India's economic growth.
Critically assess the implementation challenges of NEP 2020 at the state and local levels and suggest solutions.
articles.imageDescription: Students engaged in hands-on vocational training in a workshop setting.
articles.trivia: The Kothari Commission (1964-66) was the first to recommend a common education structure (10+2+3) for India.

articles.headline: Major Corridor of Bharatmala Pariyojana Inaugurated, Enhancing Connectivity and Logistics Efficiency
articles.explainer:
• A significant section of a national economic corridor under the Bharatmala Pariyojana was inaugurated, promising improved connectivity and reduced travel times.
• The new stretch is expected to boost trade, facilitate faster movement of goods, and integrate remote regions into the national economic mainstream.
• The project involved advanced engineering techniques, including elevated corridors and greenfield alignments, to ensure sustainable development.
• This infrastructure upgrade is crucial for enhancing India's logistics efficiency, reducing transportation costs, and supporting industrial growth.
• The Ministry of Road Transport and Highways highlighted the project's role in job creation and overall regional development.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
articles.source: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: Bharatmala Pariyojana is a centrally sponsored and funded road and highway project.
It aims to improve connectivity, especially along borders and coastal areas.
Economic corridors are a key component of the project.
NHAI is the implementing agency for national highways.
The project also focuses on reducing logistics costs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and significance of the Bharatmala Pariyojana in transforming India's infrastructure landscape.
Analyze the economic benefits of enhanced road connectivity, including its impact on trade, industry, and regional development.
Evaluate the challenges involved in large-scale infrastructure projects like land acquisition, environmental clearances, and funding.
Examine how improved logistics infrastructure can boost India's competitiveness and improve its Logistics Performance Index ranking.
Critically assess the role of public-private partnerships in accelerating infrastructure development in the country.
articles.imageDescription: A newly constructed multi-lane highway stretching through a rural landscape.
articles.trivia: India has the second-largest road network in the world, spanning over 6.3 million kilometers.

articles.headline: 'Nari Shakti Udyam Yojana' Launched to Empower Rural Women Entrepreneurs
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development launched the 'Nari Shakti Udyam Yojana', a new scheme aimed at fostering entrepreneurship among rural women.
• The scheme provides financial assistance, skill training, and market linkages to women-led micro-enterprises in various sectors like handicrafts, food processing, and digital services.
• It emphasizes building self-help groups (SHGs) and cooperatives to create a supportive ecosystem for women entrepreneurs.
• The initiative seeks to enhance women's economic independence, reduce gender disparity, and contribute to rural economic development.
• Collaborations with NABARD, SIDBI, and various NGOs are envisioned to provide comprehensive support to beneficiaries.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Role of women and women’s organization.
articles.source: Ministry of Women and Child Development / PIB
prelimsPoints: Nari Shakti Udyam Yojana is a central government scheme.
Focuses on rural women entrepreneurs.
Provides financial aid, skill training, and market linkages.
Self-Help Groups (SHGs) are a key component.
NABARD and SIDBI are crucial financial institutions for rural development and small industries.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of promoting women entrepreneurship in rural areas for inclusive growth and gender equality.
Analyze the key features of the 'Nari Shakti Udyam Yojana' and its potential impact on women's economic empowerment.
Evaluate the role of Self-Help Groups (SHGs) in mobilizing rural women and fostering entrepreneurial spirit.
Examine the challenges faced by rural women entrepreneurs and how government schemes can address these barriers.
Suggest measures to strengthen market linkages and financial access for women-led enterprises in the informal sector.
articles.imageDescription: Group of rural women engaged in skill training, possibly weaving or crafting.
articles.trivia: India has one of the largest networks of Self-Help Groups (SHGs) globally, with millions of women members.

articles.headline: India Joins Global Initiative for Resilient Supply Chains, Boosts Economic Diplomacy
articles.explainer:
• India has formally joined a new global initiative aimed at building resilient and diversified supply chains to mitigate future economic disruptions.
• The initiative, involving key global trading partners, focuses on reducing over-reliance on single-source countries and enhancing transparency in supply networks.
• India's participation underscores its commitment to strengthening global economic cooperation and ensuring stability in international trade.
• Discussions centered on developing early warning systems for supply chain disruptions and promoting investment in strategic sectors.
• This move is expected to boost India's role in global manufacturing and integrate it more deeply into diverse value chains.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
articles.source: Ministry of Commerce and Industry / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: Supply chain disruptions became prominent during the COVID-19 pandemic.
India aims to reduce dependence on specific countries for critical goods.
Supply Chain Resilience Initiative (SCRI) was launched by India, Japan, and Australia.
Diversification of supply chains is a key strategy for economic security.
India is promoting 'Make in India' for global supply chains.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of resilient supply chains for global economic stability and India's strategic interests.
Analyze the role of international cooperation and multilateral initiatives in building diversified supply networks.
Evaluate India's potential to emerge as a key hub in global supply chains, considering its manufacturing capabilities and market size.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for Indian industries in integrating into and benefiting from resilient global value chains.
Critically assess the implications of supply chain diversification for geopolitical dynamics and economic dependencies.
articles.imageDescription: Interconnected global map with arrows indicating flow of goods and supply chains.
articles.trivia: The concept of 'Just-in-Time' (JIT) manufacturing, which relies on lean supply chains, originated in Japan in the 1970s.

articles.headline: India Develops Novel Vaccine for Dengue, Clinical Trials Show Promising Results
articles.explainer:
• Indian scientists and pharmaceutical companies have announced the successful development of a novel vaccine candidate for dengue, showing promising results in advanced clinical trials.
• The indigenous vaccine targets all four serotypes of the dengue virus, offering broad protection against the vector-borne disease.
• This breakthrough is significant for India, which bears a heavy burden of dengue cases annually, particularly in urban and semi-urban areas.
• The development highlights India's growing capabilities in vaccine research and development, contributing to global health security.
• Regulatory approval is anticipated soon, paving the way for mass production and widespread deployment to combat the disease.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health.
articles.source: Department of Biotechnology / The Times of India
prelimsPoints: Dengue is a mosquito-borne viral infection.
It is caused by the dengue virus (DENV), with four distinct serotypes (DENV-1, DENV-2, DENV-3, and DENV-4).
Aedes aegypti is the primary vector for dengue.
India has a high incidence of dengue cases.
DCGI is the national regulatory authority for pharmaceuticals and medical devices in India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the public health significance of developing an indigenous dengue vaccine for India and globally.
Analyze the scientific and technological advancements that enabled the development of this novel vaccine.
Evaluate the challenges in vaccine development for vector-borne diseases like dengue and how they were overcome.
Examine the potential impact of widespread dengue vaccination on public health infrastructure and economic productivity in India.
Critically assess India's role in global vaccine research and manufacturing, drawing lessons from recent successes.
articles.imageDescription: Microscopic image of the dengue virus or a researcher working in a lab.
articles.trivia: Dengue fever is also known as 'breakbone fever' due to the severe muscle and joint pains it can cause.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Significant Buddhist Relics in Bihar
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a significant cache of ancient Buddhist relics during excavations in a historical site in Bihar.
• The findings include remnants of stupas, monastic complexes, pottery, and sculptures dating back to the Gupta period and possibly earlier.
• These discoveries shed new light on the spread of Buddhism in ancient Magadha and the architectural styles of the era.
• The site is believed to be a previously undocumented major Buddhist learning center, complementing existing knowledge about Nalanda and Vikramshila.
• The ASI plans further excavations and conservation efforts to preserve the site and study its historical importance.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
The Freedom Struggle — its various stages and important contributors/contributions from different parts of the country.
articles.source: Archaeological Survey of India / The Hindu
prelimsPoints: ASI is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage.
Bihar was the heartland of ancient Magadha, where Buddhism originated.
Gupta period (c. 320 to 550 CE) is often called the 'Golden Age of India'.
Nalanda and Vikramshila were major Buddhist universities in ancient Bihar.
Stupas are dome-shaped structures containing relics of the Buddha or Buddhist saints.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the recent archaeological discoveries in Bihar for understanding the history and spread of Buddhism in ancient India.
Analyze the contributions of the Gupta period to Indian art, architecture, and religious development, particularly in relation to Buddhism.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the Archaeological Survey of India in preserving and promoting India's rich cultural heritage.
Examine the potential of such archaeological sites to boost cultural tourism and regional economic development.
Critically assess the methods and technologies used in modern archaeological excavations and their impact on historical research.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeological excavation site showing ancient brick structures and pottery fragments.
articles.trivia: The Great Stupa at Sanchi, Madhya Pradesh, is one of the oldest stone structures in India and was originally commissioned by Emperor Ashoka in the 3rd century BCE.

articles.headline: Coastal States Enhance Preparedness for Cyclonic Season with Advanced Warning Systems
articles.explainer:
• Several Indian coastal states, particularly Odisha, West Bengal, and Andhra Pradesh, have significantly upgraded their disaster preparedness mechanisms ahead of the cyclonic season.
• The enhancements include deploying advanced Doppler weather radars, improving early warning dissemination systems, and strengthening evacuation infrastructure.
• Focus is also on community-level preparedness through awareness campaigns and training programs for local disaster response teams.
• The National Disaster Response Force (NDRF) and State Disaster Response Forces (SDRF) are conducting drills and prepositioning resources.
• These measures aim to minimize loss of life and property by ensuring timely response and effective management of cyclonic events.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management.
Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: India's eastern coast is highly vulnerable to cyclones.
IMD is the primary agency for meteorological forecasting in India.
NDRF is a specialized force for disaster response.
Doppler radars help in tracking weather systems and predicting intensity.
Odisha is often cited for its effective cyclone management model.
mainsPoints: Discuss the increasing frequency and intensity of cyclones in the Bay of Bengal and their impact on India's coastal regions.
Analyze the multi-pronged approach adopted by coastal states and central agencies to enhance cyclone preparedness and disaster management.
Evaluate the effectiveness of early warning systems and community-based disaster risk reduction strategies in mitigating cyclone damage.
Examine the challenges in evacuating populations and providing relief in cyclone-affected areas.
Suggest measures for long-term climate resilience and adaptation strategies for India's vulnerable coastal communities.
articles.imageDescription: Satellite image showing a cyclone forming over the Bay of Bengal.
articles.trivia: The word 'cyclone' was coined by Henry Piddington, a British captain, in 1840, deriving from the Greek word 'kyklos' meaning a 'coiling snake'.

articles.headline: GST Council Deliberates on Revenue Sharing Mechanisms for State Finances Amidst Fiscal Federalism Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Goods and Services Tax (GST) Council convened to discuss potential adjustments to revenue sharing mechanisms between the Centre and states, particularly concerning compensation cess.
• States raised concerns about the expiry of the GST compensation cess regime and its impact on their fiscal autonomy and revenue stability.
• Deliberations included proposals for an alternative mechanism to compensate states for revenue shortfalls and a review of the GST rate structure for certain goods and services.
• The Council also addressed issues related to simplifying compliance for small businesses and streamlining the GST appellate tribunal process.
• The discussions highlight ongoing challenges in India's fiscal federalism under the GST regime and the need for consensus-based solutions.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
articles.source: Ministry of Finance / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: GST was implemented in India on July 1, 2017.
GST Council is a constitutional body (Article 279A).
The Union Finance Minister is the chairperson of the GST Council.
GST Compensation Cess was introduced for 5 years (2017-2022) to compensate states for revenue loss.
Fiscal federalism refers to the division of financial powers and responsibilities between different levels of government.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of fiscal federalism in India under the GST regime and the challenges it presents for state finances.
Analyze the implications of the expiry of the GST compensation cess for states and the need for alternative revenue sharing mechanisms.
Evaluate the role of the GST Council in fostering cooperative federalism and achieving consensus on complex taxation issues.
Examine the impact of GST on India's economy, including its effects on tax compliance, ease of doing business, and inflation.
Critically assess the proposals for GST rate rationalization and simplification of compliance for various stakeholders.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel on a table with Indian currency notes and GST logo in the background.
articles.trivia: France was the first country to implement a Value Added Tax (VAT) system, a precursor to GST, in 1954.

highlights: India launches ambitious roadmap for Green Hydrogen adoption, targeting energy security and climate goals.
Supreme Court reinforces transparency in political funding, reiterating electoral bonds disclosure mandate.
RBI maintains repo rate amidst global uncertainties, focusing on inflation targeting in its latest monetary policy review.
Veteran labor activist Baba Adhav passes away at 95, leaving a significant legacy of social justice and workers' rights advocacy.
ISRO successfully launches advanced Earth Observation Satellite for enhanced climate monitoring and disaster management.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM) is/are correct?
1. The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
2. It primarily focuses on the mobility sector for initial adoption, excluding heavy industries.
3. The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) is the nodal ministry for the NGHM.

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
options: (a) 1 only
(b) 1 and 3 only
(c) 2 and 3 only
(d) 1, 2 and 3
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as the mission aims to leverage India's renewable energy potential for global green hydrogen leadership. Statement 2 is incorrect; the mission focuses on various sectors including steel, refineries, fertilizer, and mobility. Statement 3 is correct as MNRE is indeed the nodal ministry for the NGHM.

prelimsMocks.question:
Regarding the Supreme Court's stance on electoral bonds, which of the following is the primary principle emphasized?
(a) Protection of donor privacy to encourage political donations.
(b) Paramountcy of the voter's right to information for transparency.
(c) Ensuring equal funding for all political parties.
(d) Strengthening the role of the Election Commission in political campaigning.
options: (a) Protection of donor privacy to encourage political donations.
(b) Paramountcy of the voter's right to information for transparency.
(c) Ensuring equal funding for all political parties.
(d) Strengthening the role of the Election Commission in political campaigning.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Paramountcy of the voter's right to information for transparency.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court has consistently emphasized the voter's right to know the sources of political funding as a crucial aspect of transparency and free and fair elections, particularly in its rulings on electoral bonds.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements about the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC):
1. The MPC consists of five members, all appointed by the Government of India.
2. The primary objective of the MPC is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
3. The repo rate is the rate at which commercial banks borrow money from the RBI for short-term needs.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options: (a) 1 and 2 only
(b) 2 and 3 only
(c) 3 only
(d) 1, 2 and 3
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; the MPC consists of six members (three from RBI and three appointed by the GoI). Statement 2 is correct, as price stability with growth consideration is the mandated objective. Statement 3 is also correct, defining the repo rate.

prelimsMocks.question:
Baba Adhav, who recently passed away, was primarily associated with which of the following fields?
(a) Environmental conservation in the Western Ghats.
(b) Space research and satellite technology.
(c) Labor rights and social justice for unorganized workers.
(d) Indigenous vaccine development for tropical diseases.
options: (a) Environmental conservation in the Western Ghats.
(b) Space research and satellite technology.
(c) Labor rights and social justice for unorganized workers.
(d) Indigenous vaccine development for tropical diseases.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Labor rights and social justice for unorganized workers.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Baba Adhav was a renowned social and labor activist, particularly known for his work with unorganized laborers and his advocacy for social justice in Maharashtra.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements regarding the Western Ghats is NOT correct?
(a) It is one of the world's eight 'hottest' biodiversity hotspots.
(b) The Malabar Civet and Lion-tailed Macaque are endemic to this region.
(c) It is older than the Himalayan mountain range.
(d) It is primarily known for its desert ecosystems and arid flora.
options: (a) It is one of the world's eight 'hottest' biodiversity hotspots.
(b) The Malabar Civet and Lion-tailed Macaque are endemic to this region.
(c) It is older than the Himalayan mountain range.
(d) It is primarily known for its desert ecosystems and arid flora.
prelimsMocks.answer: (d) It is primarily known for its desert ecosystems and arid flora.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Western Ghats is known for its rich biodiversity, dense forests, and tropical ecosystems, not desert ecosystems or arid flora. Statements (a), (b), and (c) are all correct facts about the Western Ghats.

mainsMocks.question: Analyze the strategic importance of India's National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM) in achieving energy security and climate goals. Discuss the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India. (15 Marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Environmental pollution and degradation; Conservation, environmental impact assessment.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce NGHM and its dual objective. Discuss 'strategic importance' (energy independence, reduced imports, job creation, global leadership). Detail 'challenges' (cost, infrastructure, technology, storage). Outline 'opportunities' (renewable potential, policy support, export market). Conclude with a forward-looking statement on sustainable development.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's repeated emphasis on transparency in political funding, particularly concerning electoral bonds, underscores a critical aspect of democratic accountability. Discuss the implications of these rulings for India's electoral system and the broader framework of fiscal federalism. (15 Marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution; Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States; Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
mainsMocks.approach: Start with the SC's rulings on electoral bonds and the principle of transparency. Discuss implications for electoral system (voter's right to know, level playing field, reducing corruption). Connect to fiscal federalism challenges (GST compensation cess, state revenue autonomy). Conclude on strengthening democratic institutions and balancing central-state finances.

mainsMocks.question: Critically evaluate the 'Make in India' initiative in the defense sector, using the induction of INS Vagir as an example. What further measures are needed to achieve true indigenization and reduce reliance on foreign military imports? (10 Marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Security challenges; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life; Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce 'Make in India' in defense and INS Vagir's significance. Evaluate successes (design, manufacturing capabilities, job creation) and challenges (technology transfer, R&D investment, private sector involvement). Suggest further measures (long-term policy, FDI, MSME integration, testing infrastructure, export promotion). Conclude on achieving strategic autonomy.

quickBytes.text: India's National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to reduce fossil fuel imports by ₹1 lakh crore by 2030.
Article 279A of the Indian Constitution deals with the Goods and Services Tax (GST) Council.
Aedes aegypti mosquito is the primary vector responsible for transmitting the dengue virus.
The Western Ghats were declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2012.
Project 75 involves the construction of six Scorpene-class submarines for the Indian Navy.
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) was nationalized in 1949.
The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 replaced the 1986 policy.
The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) was founded in 1861 by Alexander Cunningham.
Bharatmala Pariyojana is a centrally sponsored road and highway project focusing on economic corridors.
Self-Help Groups (SHGs) are an important tool for financial inclusion and women empowerment in rural India.

GS1.headline: Veteran Labor Activist Baba Adhav Passes Away at 95, Leaves Legacy of Social Justice
GS1.explainer:
• Noted social and labor activist Baba Adhav passed away at the age of 95, leaving behind a profound legacy of advocating for the rights of unorganized workers and the marginalized.
• A staunch proponent of social justice, Adhav dedicated his life to organizing and empowering rickshaw pullers, headload workers, and other informal sector laborers in Maharashtra.
• He was a prominent figure in various social movements, including the anti-liquor movement and campaigns against exploitation of the poor.
• Adhav was also a prolific author, using his writings to articulate the struggles and demands of the working class and promote secular values.
• His demise marks the end of an era for grassroots activism and a significant loss for the social justice movement in India.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Effects of globalization on Indian society.
Social empowerment, communalism, regionalism & secularism.
prelimsPoints: Baba Adhav was known for his work with unorganized laborers.
He primarily worked in Maharashtra.
His activism covered labor rights, anti-liquor movements, and social justice.
He was also an author.
His work is relevant to the study of social movements in post-independence India.
mainsPoints: Discuss the contributions of Baba Adhav to the labor movement and social justice in India.
Analyze the challenges faced by unorganized sector workers in India and the role of activists like Adhav in addressing them.
Evaluate the effectiveness of grassroots social movements in bringing about policy changes and social empowerment.
Examine the enduring relevance of secular values and social equity in contemporary Indian society, as championed by Adhav.
Critically assess the impact of eminent personalities on shaping public discourse and government policies related to marginalized sections.
GS1.imageDescription: Black and white portrait of Baba Adhav.
GS1.trivia: The first organized labor strike in India occurred in 1877 at the Empress Mills, Nagpur, over wage issues.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Significant Buddhist Relics in Bihar
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a significant cache of ancient Buddhist relics during excavations in a historical site in Bihar.
• The findings include remnants of stupas, monastic complexes, pottery, and sculptures dating back to the Gupta period and possibly earlier.
• These discoveries shed new light on the spread of Buddhism in ancient Magadha and the architectural styles of the era.
• The site is believed to be a previously undocumented major Buddhist learning center, complementing existing knowledge about Nalanda and Vikramshila.
• The ASI plans further excavations and conservation efforts to preserve the site and study its historical importance.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
The Freedom Struggle — its various stages and important contributors/contributions from different parts of the country.
GS1.source: Archaeological Survey of India / The Hindu
prelimsPoints: ASI is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage.
Bihar was the heartland of ancient Magadha, where Buddhism originated.
Gupta period (c. 320 to 550 CE) is often called the 'Golden Age of India'.
Nalanda and Vikramshila were major Buddhist universities in ancient Bihar.
Stupas are dome-shaped structures containing relics of the Buddha or Buddhist saints.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the recent archaeological discoveries in Bihar for understanding the history and spread of Buddhism in ancient India.
Analyze the contributions of the Gupta period to Indian art, architecture, and religious development, particularly in relation to Buddhism.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the Archaeological Survey of India in preserving and promoting India's rich cultural heritage.
Examine the potential of such archaeological sites to boost cultural tourism and regional economic development.
Critically assess the methods and technologies used in modern archaeological excavations and their impact on historical research.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeological excavation site showing ancient brick structures and pottery fragments.
GS1.trivia: The Great Stupa at Sanchi, Madhya Pradesh, is one of the oldest stone structures in India and was originally commissioned by Emperor Ashoka in the 3rd century BCE.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Transparency in Electoral Funding, Reiterates Disclosure Mandate
GS2.explainer:
• In a landmark ruling, the Supreme Court of India reinforced the necessity of transparency in political funding by upholding the disclosure requirements related to electoral bonds.
• The judgment emphasized that the right to information of the voter is paramount, ensuring that citizens are aware of the sources of political party funding.
• The ruling addressed petitions seeking clarity and enforcement of previous directives regarding the complete disclosure of electoral bond data.
• It directed the Election Commission of India (ECI) to ensure the timely publication of all donor and recipient details, without any obfuscation.
• This decision is expected to strengthen democratic processes and accountability in political financing.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
prelimsPoints: Electoral Bonds Scheme was introduced in 2017.
Supreme Court previously struck down anonymity in electoral bonds in February 2024.
Right to Information is a fundamental right under Article 19(1)(a).
Election Commission of India (ECI) is responsible for regulating elections.
ADR is a key petitioner in electoral funding cases.
mainsPoints: Analyze the impact of the Supreme Court's verdict on transparency and accountability in India's electoral system.
Discuss the arguments for and against the anonymity of electoral bonds in the context of political funding and donor privacy.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in strengthening democratic institutions and ensuring fair elections in India.
Examine the challenges in implementing complete transparency in political funding and suggest further reforms.
Compare India's electoral finance regulations with global best practices and identify areas for improvement.
GS2.imageDescription: Image of the Supreme Court of India building.
GS2.trivia: The first general elections in independent India were held from 1951 to 1952, marking the largest democratic exercise at the time.

GS2.headline: India-EU Summit Strengthens Strategic Partnership with Focus on Digital and Green Transition
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-European Union (EU) Strategic Partnership Dialogue concluded with both sides reaffirming their commitment to a robust and comprehensive partnership.
• Key outcomes included enhanced cooperation on digital technologies, including AI, cybersecurity, and data governance, alongside joint efforts on green energy transition.
• Discussions also covered trade and investment facilitation, supply chain resilience, and collaboration on critical raw materials.
• Leaders emphasized the importance of a free, open, and rules-based Indo-Pacific, with India and the EU playing crucial roles.
• Agreements were signed to boost research and innovation, particularly in areas like sustainable urban development and clean energy technologies.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
GS2.source: Ministry of External Affairs / The Times of India
prelimsPoints: India and EU established a Strategic Partnership in 2004.
EU is India's third-largest trading partner.
Both sides are negotiating a Free Trade Agreement (FTA).
Key areas of cooperation: trade, climate, digital, security.
EU's Global Gateway strategy aims to counter China's BRI.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of the India-EU partnership in the current geopolitical landscape.
Discuss the potential for cooperation between India and the EU in driving the global digital and green transitions.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in accelerating the India-EU Free Trade Agreement negotiations.
Examine how India and the EU can collaborate to promote a stable and prosperous Indo-Pacific region.
Assess the role of multilateralism and rules-based order in the India-EU joint vision.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union displayed together during a summit.
GS2.trivia: The European Union is a unique economic and political union of 27 member states, covering much of the continent.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Focus on Skill Development and Vocational Training for Youth
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a progress report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, highlighting significant strides in integrating skill development.
• The report emphasized the expansion of vocational education from school to higher education levels, with a focus on practical, industry-relevant skills.
• New guidelines were issued for collaboration between educational institutions and industries to provide apprenticeships and on-the-job training opportunities.
• The policy aims to bridge the skill gap in the workforce, enhance employability, and foster an entrepreneurial mindset among the youth.
• Efforts are underway to align curriculum with National Skills Qualification Framework (NSQF) and introduce credit-based vocational courses.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
GS2.source: Ministry of Education / Press Information Bureau (PIB)
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
One of its key pillars is vocational education integration.
NSQF organizes qualifications by a series of levels of knowledge, skills, and aptitude.
The policy aims for a multidisciplinary and holistic education system.
GER in higher education is a key target metric for NEP.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of NEP 2020 in addressing India's skill gap and enhancing youth employability.
Discuss the challenges in integrating vocational education effectively into the mainstream curriculum from school to higher education.
Evaluate the role of industry-academia collaboration in making skill development programs more relevant and outcome-oriented.
Examine how NEP 2020 can foster an entrepreneurial ecosystem and contribute to India's economic growth.
Critically assess the implementation challenges of NEP 2020 at the state and local levels and suggest solutions.
GS2.imageDescription: Students engaged in hands-on vocational training in a workshop setting.
GS2.trivia: The Kothari Commission (1964-66) was the first to recommend a common education structure (10+2+3) for India.

GS2.headline: 'Nari Shakti Udyam Yojana' Launched to Empower Rural Women Entrepreneurs
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development launched the 'Nari Shakti Udyam Yojana', a new scheme aimed at fostering entrepreneurship among rural women.
• The scheme provides financial assistance, skill training, and market linkages to women-led micro-enterprises in various sectors like handicrafts, food processing, and digital services.
• It emphasizes building self-help groups (SHGs) and cooperatives to create a supportive ecosystem for women entrepreneurs.
• The initiative seeks to enhance women's economic independence, reduce gender disparity, and contribute to rural economic development.
• Collaborations with NABARD, SIDBI, and various NGOs are envisioned to provide comprehensive support to beneficiaries.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Role of women and women’s organization.
GS2.source: Ministry of Women and Child Development / PIB
prelimsPoints: Nari Shakti Udyam Yojana is a central government scheme.
Focuses on rural women entrepreneurs.
Provides financial aid, skill training, and market linkages.
Self-Help Groups (SHGs) are a key component.
NABARD and SIDBI are crucial financial institutions for rural development and small industries.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of promoting women entrepreneurship in rural areas for inclusive growth and gender equality.
Analyze the key features of the 'Nari Shakti Udyam Yojana' and its potential impact on women's economic empowerment.
Evaluate the role of Self-Help Groups (SHGs) in mobilizing rural women and fostering entrepreneurial spirit.
Examine the challenges faced by rural women entrepreneurs and how government schemes can address these barriers.
Suggest measures to strengthen market linkages and financial access for women-led enterprises in the informal sector.
GS2.imageDescription: Group of rural women engaged in skill training, possibly weaving or crafting.
GS2.trivia: India has one of the largest networks of Self-Help Groups (SHGs) globally, with millions of women members.

GS2.headline: India Joins Global Initiative for Resilient Supply Chains, Boosts Economic Diplomacy
GS2.explainer:
• India has formally joined a new global initiative aimed at building resilient and diversified supply chains to mitigate future economic disruptions.
• The initiative, involving key global trading partners, focuses on reducing over-reliance on single-source countries and enhancing transparency in supply networks.
• India's participation underscores its commitment to strengthening global economic cooperation and ensuring stability in international trade.
• Discussions centered on developing early warning systems for supply chain disruptions and promoting investment in strategic sectors.
• This move is expected to boost India's role in global manufacturing and integrate it more deeply into diverse value chains.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
GS2.source: Ministry of Commerce and Industry / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: Supply chain disruptions became prominent during the COVID-19 pandemic.
India aims to reduce dependence on specific countries for critical goods.
Supply Chain Resilience Initiative (SCRI) was launched by India, Japan, and Australia.
Diversification of supply chains is a key strategy for economic security.
India is promoting 'Make in India' for global supply chains.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of resilient supply chains for global economic stability and India's strategic interests.
Analyze the role of international cooperation and multilateral initiatives in building diversified supply networks.
Evaluate India's potential to emerge as a key hub in global supply chains, considering its manufacturing capabilities and market size.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for Indian industries in integrating into and benefiting from resilient global value chains.
Critically assess the implications of supply chain diversification for geopolitical dynamics and economic dependencies.
GS2.imageDescription: Interconnected global map with arrows indicating flow of goods and supply chains.
GS2.trivia: The concept of 'Just-in-Time' (JIT) manufacturing, which relies on lean supply chains, originated in Japan in the 1970s.

GS2.headline: GST Council Deliberates on Revenue Sharing Mechanisms for State Finances Amidst Fiscal Federalism Concerns
GS2.explainer:
• The Goods and Services Tax (GST) Council convened to discuss potential adjustments to revenue sharing mechanisms between the Centre and states, particularly concerning compensation cess.
• States raised concerns about the expiry of the GST compensation cess regime and its impact on their fiscal autonomy and revenue stability.
• Deliberations included proposals for an alternative mechanism to compensate states for revenue shortfalls and a review of the GST rate structure for certain goods and services.
• The Council also addressed issues related to simplifying compliance for small businesses and streamlining the GST appellate tribunal process.
• The discussions highlight ongoing challenges in India's fiscal federalism under the GST regime and the need for consensus-based solutions.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
GS2.source: Ministry of Finance / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: GST was implemented in India on July 1, 2017.
GST Council is a constitutional body (Article 279A).
The Union Finance Minister is the chairperson of the GST Council.
GST Compensation Cess was introduced for 5 years (2017-2022) to compensate states for revenue loss.
Fiscal federalism refers to the division of financial powers and responsibilities between different levels of government.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of fiscal federalism in India under the GST regime and the challenges it presents for state finances.
Analyze the implications of the expiry of the GST compensation cess for states and the need for alternative revenue sharing mechanisms.
Evaluate the role of the GST Council in fostering cooperative federalism and achieving consensus on complex taxation issues.
Examine the impact of GST on India's economy, including its effects on tax compliance, ease of doing business, and inflation.
Critically assess the proposals for GST rate rationalization and simplification of compliance for various stakeholders.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel on a table with Indian currency notes and GST logo in the background.
GS2.trivia: France was the first country to implement a Value Added Tax (VAT) system, a precursor to GST, in 1954.

GS3.headline: India Unveils Ambitious Roadmap for Green Hydrogen Adoption, Targets Energy Security
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) released a detailed roadmap outlining strategies for scaling up green hydrogen production and consumption across various sectors.
• The plan emphasizes incentives for domestic manufacturing of electrolysers, development of green hydrogen hubs, and R&D for cost reduction.
• Focus areas include steel, refineries, fertilizer, and mobility sectors for initial adoption, aiming to reduce dependence on fossil fuels.
• The roadmap also highlights India's potential to become a global hub for green hydrogen export, leveraging its renewable energy resources.
• Public-private partnerships and international collaborations are key components for achieving the mission's ambitious targets.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM) launched in 2023.
Target sectors: steel, refineries, fertilizer, mobility.
Key component: domestic manufacturing of electrolysers.
MNRE is the nodal ministry for NGHM.
Goal is to reduce fossil fuel imports and achieve net-zero targets.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Evaluate the role of government policies and private investment in fostering a robust green hydrogen ecosystem.
Examine the potential economic benefits, including job creation and export opportunities, from the NGHM.
Critically assess the technological hurdles and infrastructure requirements for widespread green hydrogen implementation.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Global Headwinds, Focuses on Inflation Targeting
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly review, deciding to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at X%.
• The decision was influenced by persistent global economic uncertainties, geopolitical tensions, and the need to anchor domestic inflation expectations.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth, maintaining an 'accommodative stance with withdrawal of accommodation'.
• RBI Governor highlighted the resilience of the Indian economy but cautioned about the potential impact of crude oil prices and supply chain disruptions.
• Future policy actions will remain data-dependent, with a close watch on inflation trajectory and growth momentum.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
GS3.source: Reserve Bank of India / Business Standard
prelimsPoints: Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of 6 members (3 RBI, 3 GoI).
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
Inflation target set by the government in consultation with RBI is 4% (+/- 2%).
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
'Withdrawal of accommodation' stance indicates focus on reducing liquidity.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its implications for the Indian economy.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with the objective of supporting economic growth.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in addressing supply-side inflation and global economic shocks.
Examine the transmission mechanism of monetary policy in India and the role of commercial banks.
Critically assess the 'accommodative stance with withdrawal of accommodation' and its future trajectory.
GS3.imageDescription: Facade of the Reserve Bank of India building in Mumbai.
GS3.trivia: The RBI was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, initially headquartered in Kolkata.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite for Climate Monitoring
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched a new generation Earth Observation Satellite (EOS) from Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota.
• The satellite, named EOS-XX, is equipped with advanced remote sensing instruments designed to monitor climate change indicators, agricultural health, and disaster preparedness.
• This mission is crucial for enhancing India's capabilities in environmental monitoring, resource management, and providing critical data for meteorological forecasts.
• The satellite will provide high-resolution imagery and data, aiding in understanding land-use patterns, forest cover changes, and water resource management.
• The launch further solidifies India's position in global space technology and its commitment to utilizing space for societal benefits.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: ISRO is India's national space agency.
Earth Observation Satellites (EOS) are used for monitoring Earth's surface.
Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC) is India's primary spaceport.
Remote sensing involves collecting information about an object or area without being in direct contact with it.
PSLV and GSLV are ISRO's operational launch vehicles.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites for India's climate resilience and resource management.
Analyze how ISRO's advancements in space technology contribute to India's developmental goals and global standing.
Evaluate the role of remote sensing data in disaster prediction, early warning systems, and post-disaster assessment.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India in developing and deploying advanced space-based applications.
Critically assess the importance of indigenous space capabilities for national security and economic growth.
GS3.imageDescription: Rocket launching from a launchpad with smoke trails, against a clear sky.
GS3.trivia: India's first satellite, Aryabhata, was launched on April 19, 1975, by the Soviet Union.

GS3.headline: New Conservation Plan Rolled Out for Critically Endangered Species in Western Ghats
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) launched a comprehensive conservation plan targeting critically endangered species in the Western Ghats.
• The initiative focuses on habitat restoration, anti-poaching measures, community participation, and scientific research for species like the Malabar Civet and Lion-tailed Macaque.
• The plan integrates traditional ecological knowledge with modern conservation techniques, involving local communities as key stakeholders.
• Funding will be allocated for establishing new wildlife corridors, reducing human-wildlife conflict, and strengthening protected area management.
• This move underscores India's commitment to biodiversity preservation in one of the world's top biodiversity hotspots.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change / Down To Earth
prelimsPoints: Western Ghats is one of 8 global biodiversity hotspots.
Malabar Civet and Lion-tailed Macaque are endemic to Western Ghats.
IUCN Red List classifies species based on extinction risk.
Wildlife Protection Act, 1972 is the primary law for wildlife conservation in India.
Community participation is crucial for successful conservation efforts.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats and the threats it faces from anthropogenic activities.
Analyze the key features of the new conservation plan and its potential effectiveness in protecting endangered species.
Evaluate the role of community participation and traditional knowledge in biodiversity conservation efforts.
Examine the challenges in implementing conservation strategies in diverse and densely populated regions like the Western Ghats.
Suggest additional measures to strengthen biodiversity protection and mitigate human-wildlife conflict in India.
GS3.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with dense forests and a waterfall.
GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats is older than the Himalayas, and its forests influence the Indian monsoon weather patterns.

GS3.headline: Indigenous Scorpene-Class Submarine 'Vagir' Inducted into Indian Navy, Boosting Maritime Security
GS3.explainer:
• The fifth Kalvari-class (Scorpene-class) submarine, INS Vagir, was formally commissioned into the Indian Navy, significantly enhancing India's underwater warfare capabilities.
• Built under Project 75 by Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Limited (MDL) in collaboration with Naval Group of France, Vagir demonstrates India's growing self-reliance in defense manufacturing.
• The submarine is designed for anti-surface warfare, anti-submarine warfare, intelligence gathering, mine laying, and area surveillance.
• Its induction is part of a broader strategy to modernize the Indian Navy and strengthen its presence in the Indian Ocean Region.
• This development underscores the 'Make in India' initiative in defense and India's commitment to becoming a net security provider in the region.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: INS Vagir is a Scorpene-class submarine.
It is built under Project 75.
MDL is the primary builder in India.
Naval Group of France is the collaborator.
Scorpene submarines are conventional diesel-electric attack submarines.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous submarine construction for India's maritime security and strategic autonomy.
Analyze the role of the Indian Navy in safeguarding India's economic interests and maintaining stability in the Indian Ocean Region.
Evaluate the progress and challenges of the 'Make in India' initiative in the defense sector, using Project 75 as an example.
Examine the geopolitical implications of strengthening India's underwater warfare capabilities for regional power dynamics.
Suggest measures to further accelerate defense indigenization and reduce reliance on foreign military imports.
GS3.imageDescription: INS Vagir submarine sailing on the sea surface with naval personnel on board.
GS3.trivia: The first Indian Navy submarine, INS Kalvari (F-class), was commissioned on December 8, 1967, from the Soviet Union.

GS3.headline: Major Corridor of Bharatmala Pariyojana Inaugurated, Enhancing Connectivity and Logistics Efficiency
GS3.explainer:
• A significant section of a national economic corridor under the Bharatmala Pariyojana was inaugurated, promising improved connectivity and reduced travel times.
• The new stretch is expected to boost trade, facilitate faster movement of goods, and integrate remote regions into the national economic mainstream.
• The project involved advanced engineering techniques, including elevated corridors and greenfield alignments, to ensure sustainable development.
• This infrastructure upgrade is crucial for enhancing India's logistics efficiency, reducing transportation costs, and supporting industrial growth.
• The Ministry of Road Transport and Highways highlighted the project's role in job creation and overall regional development.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
GS3.source: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways / The Economic Times
prelimsPoints: Bharatmala Pariyojana is a centrally sponsored and funded road and highway project.
It aims to improve connectivity, especially along borders and coastal areas.
Economic corridors are a key component of the project.
NHAI is the implementing agency for national highways.
The project also focuses on reducing logistics costs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and significance of the Bharatmala Pariyojana in transforming India's infrastructure landscape.
Analyze the economic benefits of enhanced road connectivity, including its impact on trade, industry, and regional development.
Evaluate the challenges involved in large-scale infrastructure projects like land acquisition, environmental clearances, and funding.
Examine how improved logistics infrastructure can boost India's competitiveness and improve its Logistics Performance Index ranking.
Critically assess the role of public-private partnerships in accelerating infrastructure development in the country.
GS3.imageDescription: A newly constructed multi-lane highway stretching through a rural landscape.
GS3.trivia: India has the second-largest road network in the world, spanning over 6.3 million kilometers.

GS3.headline: India Develops Novel Vaccine for Dengue, Clinical Trials Show Promising Results
GS3.explainer:
